visual_checks: Tab through all interactive elements and verify the focused element is fully visible at all times
Check if sticky headers, footers, or navigation bars cover any part of a focused element
Look for cookie banners, chat widgets, or floating action buttons that overlap focused elements
Verify that modal dialogs and overlays do not allow focus to reach elements hidden behind them
Check if scrollable containers properly scroll focused elements fully into view
Look for absolutely or fixed-positioned elements that may partially overlap focusable elements
pass_conditions: No part of any focused element is hidden or obscured by author-created content
Sticky headers and footers do not overlap any portion of focused elements
Cookie banners, chat widgets, and floating buttons do not cover any part of focused elements
Focus within scrollable regions scrolls the element completely into the visible area
When focus moves to an element behind a dialog, the dialog is dismissed or focus is trapped within the dialog
fail_conditions: A sticky header or footer partially covers a focused element
A cookie consent banner overlaps part of a focused interactive element
A chat widget or floating action button obscures a portion of a focused element
Focus moves behind a modal overlay where the focused element is partially or fully hidden
A focused element is partially scrolled out of view within a scrollable container
na_conditions: Page has no interactive elements that can receive keyboard focus
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Keyboard focus behavior could not be triggered or observed in the testing environment
common_mistakes: Sticky navigation bars that obscure the top of focused elements just below them
Cookie banners that persist at the bottom of the viewport covering focused footer links
Chat widget bubbles positioned over interactive elements in the lower-right corner
Fixed-position 'back to top' buttons that overlap focused elements
Dropdown menus or tooltips from other elements that partially cover a newly focused element

examples.pass: Tabbing through the page keeps every focused element fully visible — the sticky header accounts for scroll offset so elements beneath it are scrolled fully past it before receiving focus
examples.fail: A sticky header covers the top 20% of a focused text input field when the user tabs into a form section just below the header — part of the focused element is hidden by author content
examples.partial: Most focused elements are fully visible, but a persistent cookie banner at the bottom of the viewport partially obscures two footer links when they receive focus

plain_meaning: The stricter AAA version of focus-not-obscured: when a component receives keyboard focus, NO part of it may be hidden by author-created content. Even partial occlusion by a sticky overlay fails this SC.
scope_applies_to: Focused components that must remain fully visible
Sticky headers, footers, chat widgets, and cookie banners that could clip any portion of a focused element
Any author overlay interaction with focus position
scope_does_not_apply_to: Pages with no sticky or overlay content
Cases where only entire obscuring is possible and handled under 2.4.11
borderline_calibration: Any partial overlap with author content that clips the focused element is a failure under this stricter SC.
Satisfying 2.4.11 is NOT enough to satisfy 2.4.12.
Rely on concrete screenshot evidence, not speculation.
Not applicable if the page has no overlay content that could obscure focus.
